name: Chronarr
description: Synchronizes TV episode and movie DateCreated to preserve import dates in Jellyfin
overview: This plugin automatically syncs PremiereDate to DateCreated for TV episodes and movies to ensure Jellyfin displays the correct import date from the Chronarr database instead of scan dates.

versions.changelog: Fix: Removed TLS certificate pinning — the pinned leaf cert hash expired, breaking license check-ins for all users. License responses are already protected by RSA signature verification, making cert pinning redundant. Standard TLS CA validation is now used, which will not break on routine certificate renewals.

Fix: Crash on Jellyfin 10.11.x eliminated. The series DateLastMediaAdded updater called ILibraryManager.GetItemList() which was removed in Jellyfin 10.11; replaced with QueryItems().Items which exists in all supported versions. This was causing Jellyfin to crash with a fatal MissingMethodException every time the real-time series date debounce timer fired, preventing DateLastMediaAdded from ever being updated.

Fix: Re-added or refreshed TV series no longer float to the top of Recently Added after Chronarr corrects episode dates. The plugin now updates Series.DateLastMediaAdded to the max episode DateCreated after each real-time sync (debounced 30s per series) and at the end of each scheduled task run. Fix: Dates from the Chronarr database are now treated as UTC, preventing a 4-5 hour timezone offset that caused unnecessary re-syncs on every library refresh.

Fix: 'Missing season or episode number' no longer appears in Failed Syncs. These were always transient — caused by Jellyfin's Season Unknown state during library refreshes, not real failures. The correctly-matched episode fires its own event and syncs normally. Retry logic is preserved for genuine new-import timing races.

Fix: Real-time sync no longer floods Failed Syncs with virtual/placeholder episodes — Jellyfin guide data entries for episodes not yet on disk are now silently skipped. Fix: Real-time sync now retries up to 3 times (30s apart) when a newly-added episode or movie is missing season/episode numbers or IMDB ID due to metadata not being ready yet, preventing false failure entries for legitimate new imports. Security: Admin-only authorization added to the Failed Syncs API; IMDB IDs are validated before use in lookup URLs. Fix: License manager now disposes cleanly on plugin reload with no leaked timers or connections; uses Jellyfin's supported server-ID API. Fix: Exceptions are no longer silently dropped in several error paths.

Security: Brought license handling in line with the Emby plugin — RSA-signed validation responses, certificate pinning, a bounded/signed offline grace period, rate-limited check-ins, and platform identification. Removed unreliable anti-debug checks that could misfire on long-running servers.
Fix: Real-time sync thread affinity issue resolved; Changed async void event handler pattern to prevent silent failures
Rebranded to Chronarr with database-first architecture
